name: Utah Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL)

address.streetAddress:
160 East 300 South, 1st Floor
Salt Lake City, UT 84111

licenseDuration: Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to Sept 30 even-year cycle)
renewalCycle: Every 2 years (expires Sept 30 of even-numbered years)

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Provide a copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration wallet card.

requiredDocuments.name: Official Application
requiredDocuments.description: Completed application form with the $60 fee.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Utah dietitian certification cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial cost is a $60 application fee.

mainEntity.name: When does the Utah certification need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah Certified Dietitian credentials expire on September 30 of all even-numbered years. The renewal fee is $37.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah accepts the continuing education completed for the maintenance of your national CDR registration (75 hours every five years) to satisfy state renewal requirements.

mainEntity.name: Does Utah have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
